name: Daniel Bellinger
headline: Efficient blocker-first tight end with no real target floor
body:
Bellinger's 2025 with the Giants flashed quiet efficiency without anything resembling fantasy volume. On just 26 targets across 13 games he posted a 0.869 EPA per play, a 73.1% success rate and 1.23 yards per route run, catching 19 balls for 286 yards and two scores. The problem is everything underneath that: a 40.7% route share, a 5.2% target share and a 0.112 target-per-route-run rate cap his ceiling regardless of how clean the per-snap math looks. He moves the chains when used, but he simply isn't used enough to matter in lineups.

The move to Tennessee reunites him with Brian Daboll, now the offensive coordinator, which is the one thread worth tracking given their Giants history. Even so, the projected role is blocker-first, with the upside hinging on a target spike that hasn't materialized in three seasons. With an ADP outside pick 360 and TE56-type ranking, he stays a deep-bench dart at best until volume actually shows up.
